title: A Novel Filamentous Phage from the Deep-Sea Bacterium Shewanella piezotolerans WP3 Is Induced at Low Temperature
summary: The paper describes Shewanella phage SW1, a filamentous phage isolated from the deep-sea bacterium Shewanella piezotolerans WP3. SW1 has a 7,718 nucleotide genome with nine ORFs organized into replication, structural, assembly, and regulation modules. It exhibits temperature-regulated production, with higher phage yields at lower temperatures (4°C). SW1 infects Shewanella psychrophila WP2 and shows stability at 60°C but is inactivated at 70°C. The phage's transcription is induced by low temperature, mitomycin C, and UV irradiation, suggesting SOS control.

Shewanella phage SW1.growth_medium: 2216E medium (5 g/liter tryptone, 1 g/liter yeast extract, 0.1 g/liter FePO4, 34 g/liter NaCl)

extra.modular_structures: replication module, structural module, assembly module, regulation module
extra.transcription_induction: induced by low temperature, mitomycin C, UV irradiation
